history_events.title: Topic History: League Overview
history_events.summary: 2014 rules define 2 conferences with 2 divisions of 3 teams; the current rulebook uses 4 divisions (no conferences).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- 2014 rules define 2 conferences with 2 divisions of 3 teams; the current rulebook uses 4 divisions (no conferences).
- 2014 entry fee was $50 ($60 for new owners); current rulebook lists $200 dues.
- Competition Committee (CC) was eliminated (during the season the Baldarellis were banned); current governance is commissioner-led.
- Current rulebook adds admission quiz, two-season probation, and dispersal draft for new owners, which are not present in legacy versions.
- 2012 poll adopted a bench-player (non-QB) tiebreaker for playoff/regular-season ties; this rule was removed prior to the 2025 season by commissioner ruling.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: League hosted on MFL; 12-team salary cap league.
- 2014: 2 conferences with 2 divisions of 3 teams each; realignment every 3 years based on all-play record Weeks 1-13, with specific division groupings and .500 all-play assigned to new owners in realignment years.
- 2014: Regular season Weeks 1-13; playoffs Weeks 14-16.
- 2014: Competition Committee (Keith Creelman, Ryan Bousquet, Eric Mannila).
- 2014: Schedule uses double-headers each week (26-game regular season).
- 2014: Standings/tiebreakers defined for divisional champs, wild cards, and 1 vs 2 seeds (Pct, Div Pct, H2H, PF, All-Play, PWR, coin flip order).
- 2014: Championship/Toilet Bowl seeding and draft-pick implications are conference-based (details in 2014 rulebook).

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether legacy voting thresholds (51% off-season, 90% in-season, 75% dues) still apply under commissioner-led governance.
- Confirm whether conference-based standings/tiebreakers and double-header scheduling still apply (detailed in 2014 rulebook but not in current).
- Pin down the exact season the bench-player tiebreaker was removed (known to be pre-2025).

history_events.title: Topic History: League Calendar
history_events.summary: Season rollover moved from Feb 1 to March 1 starting with the 2014 season; later shifted to March 1-15 (2018 and current).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Season rollover moved from Feb 1 to March 1 starting with the 2014 season; later shifted to March 1-15 (2018 and current).
- Rookie Draft timing shifted from June (2014) to mid-June (2018) to Memorial Day weekend (current).
- Free Agent Auction timing shifted from Aug 1 (2014/2018) to the last weekend of July with a 3-day prior roster lock (current).
- Trade deadline moved from Week 12 (2014) and Week 11 (2013/2014 by-laws) to Thanksgiving kickoff (2018/current).
- Playoffs moved from Weeks 14-16 (2014/2018) to starting Week 15 for 3 weeks (current).
- 2018 explicitly abolished the rule that playoff-period acquisitions are ineligible to be retained; current rulebook does not mention that rule.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Off-season rule-change voting thresholds: 51% for future changes; 90% for in-season changes; non-responses excluded; CC may act unanimously.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Tagging period and deadlines are not listed in the current calendar; confirm if franchise/transition tagging still exists and when it occurs.
- Confirm the April 30 expired-rookie extension deadline supersedes older June/July dates.

history_events.title: Topic History: Roster Management
history_events.summary: Roster size increased from 23-26 (2014) to min 27 and max 30 after the contract deadline (max 35 during auction) in the current rulebook.
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Roster size increased from 23-26 (2014) to min 27 and max 30 after the contract deadline (max 35 during auction) in the current rulebook.
- Historical roster caps: 2012–2017 in-season max was 26, with auction-only overage up to 31; 2018+ in-season max is 30, with auction-only overage up to 35.
- Taxi rules changed: 2014 allowed max 9 with 5/3/1 contract-year tiers (forum vote 2014-02-11, 7–5); current uses a 10-player taxi with 1 IDP minimum and 2nd-round+ eligibility.
- Starting lineup shifted from the 2014 fixed structure to the current flex/superflex format with expanded IDP slots and a 3-starting-QB roster cap.
- Holdout/suspension handling moved from special-circumstance 25% relief options (2013/2014) to current IR rules (50% relief, rollover rules).
- 2012 poll rejected a minimum 8-IDP roster requirement (only lineup IDP requirements remained).
- 2014 forum vote (Feb 11, 2014) clarified taxi-squad cuts: players not on the active roster incur no cap charge when cut; 2nd+ round rookies cut before the contract deadline are penalty-free if never promoted; once promoted, cuts incur cap hits.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: In-season roster max 26 (auction max 31), min 23; 24 hours to fix invalid lineup after transactions; must stay under $300,000 cap.
- 2014: Off-season (post-Week 16 through Aug 1) had no roster or cap limits.
- 2014 (forum vote 2014-02-11, 7–5): Taxi squad max 9 with contract-year tiers (5 with 3 years, 3 with 2 years, 1 with 1 year), 2nd-round+ eligibility, no return once activated, Sept 1 demotion deadline.
- 2014: IR had no limit; 50% salary reduction; activation required upon return; off-season IR eligibility enforced before auction.
- 2014 (forum vote 2014-02-11): No player cut while not on the active roster incurs a cap charge. Rookies drafted in Round 2+ cut before the contract deadline are penalty-free if never promoted; any player promoted from taxi and later cut incurs a cap hit.
- 2014: Starting lineup requirements were fixed (QB, RB, 2 WR, TE, 2 Flex, DL, LB, DB, 3 Def Flex, K, P).
- 2014: Lineup warnings for bye/out/doubtful starters; MFL auto-submits if lineup not set by Thursday.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm the season when suspended players became IR-eligible (owner confirmation says this was added later).
- Confirm whether the 2014 taxi squad limits by contract years still apply or are fully replaced by the current taxi rules.
- Confirm whether holdout relief is 50% (current) or legacy 25% with Week-8 options.
- Confirm whether the 2014 taxi-squad cut/guarantee rule (no cap hit unless promoted) remains the governing standard.
- 2014 forum proposal discussed adding a mandatory second RB (lineup 1QB/2RB/2WR/1TE/2Flex) potentially effective 2015; confirm if any version was adopted.

history_events.title: Topic History: Rookie Draft
history_events.summary: Rookie contract length was 2 years in 2012, moved to 3 years starting 2013 by league vote, then removed prior to the 2025 season by commissioner ruling for ease of management.
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Rookie contract length was 2 years in 2012, moved to 3 years starting 2013 by league vote, then removed prior to the 2025 season by commissioner ruling for ease of management.
- Rookie salary schedule evolved: legacy docs specify 3rd+ round salaries ($2K or $1K); current rulebook only lists 1st/2nd round salaries in the table.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Rookie draft is 5 rounds, non-snake, 3-minute clock with ADP auto-pick.
- 2014: Draft order determined by playoff finish (toilet bowl picks 1-6; championship picks 7-12).
- 2014: Supplemental draft uses blind bids with forfeited future picks; ties resolved by draft order.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Current rulebook does not specify rookie salaries for rounds 3+; confirm whether legacy $2K/$1K rules still apply.
- Confirm the current rookie-contract length enforcement after the pre-2025 removal (if any default length is still used).

history_events.title: Topic History: Free Agent Auction
history_events.summary: Auction format shifted from daily nominations (2014) and a 2-week auction (2018) to a 7-day minimum nomination window in late July (current).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Auction format shifted from daily nominations (2014) and a 2-week auction (2018) to a 7-day minimum nomination window in late July (current).
- Legacy docs list explicit warnings/fines for missed nominations; current rulebook does not define those penalties.
- Nomination cadence has been treated as flexible in practice (e.g., 2/day with a noon start and midnight reset, allowing multiple nominations within a 12-hour window).

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Auction begins at midnight on Aug 1; no cuts allowed during auction.
- 2014: 1 nomination per day (CC may increase after 10 days); 24-hour timer resets on new bids.
- 2014: Bids in $1K increments, $1K min; roster must end 23-31 with full lineup; cap compliance required.
- 2014: Missed nomination warnings; once roster is full, missed nominations indicate owner is done bidding.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether missed-nomination warnings/fines from 2014 still apply.

history_events.title: Topic History: Expired Rookie Auction
history_events.summary: Expired rookie extension deadlines shifted from June 1 (2013/2014) and July 1 (2018) to April 30 (current).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Expired rookie extension deadlines shifted from June 1 (2013/2014) and July 1 (2018) to April 30 (current).

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Expired rookie extensions handled before June 1; see legacy contract docs.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether the expired-rookie auction and extension deadlines are aligned with April 30 in the current doc.

history_events.title: Topic History: Waivers
history_events.summary: Blind-bid waiver run days changed from Wed/Thu/Sat/Sun (2014) to Thu/Fri/Sat/Sun (current).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Blind-bid waiver run days changed from Wed/Thu/Sat/Sun (2014) to Thu/Fri/Sat/Sun (current).
- Tie-breaker order changed from record-based (2014) to All-Play/Overall/Points/H2H (current).
- FCFS window expanded from Sunday 9 AM-1 PM (2014) to post-Sunday waiver run until kickoff (current).
- First blind-bid run timing has varied year to year (2012 poll selected first Wednesday after auction, but later seasons moved away from Wednesday runs).

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Dropped players hit waivers Monday 9:00 PM; no waivers in off-season.
- 2014: Blind bid waivers run Wed/Thu/Sat/Sun at 9 AM; $1K min and $1K increments; tie-breaker order based on record/All-Play/points/H2H; pre-season tie-breaker is draft order.
- 2014: First waiver run is first Wednesday after auction; FCFS runs Sundays Week 1-16 from 9 AM (contract $1K).

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether any rolling-waiver order or waiver-claim limits still exist alongside blind-bid waivers.

history_events.title: Topic History: Trades
history_events.summary: Trade votes were removed in 2013: commissioner processes trades; two collusion objections within 48 hours trigger a veto poll requiring 5 collusion votes.
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Trade votes were removed in 2013: commissioner processes trades; two collusion objections within 48 hours trigger a veto poll requiring 5 collusion votes. Trades involving starters must process 4 hours before kickoff, and MFL blocks trades if any involved player has already kicked off.
- Trade processing later moved from commissioner with potential veto poll (2014) to immediate processing with post-review (current).
- Current rulebook adds an asset-required rule (salary-only trades prohibited) and disallows trading Round 6 picks.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Trades processed by commissioner; veto poll triggered if two owners raise collusion concerns.
- 2014: Trades allowed for current and one-year-ahead picks; trade window March 1 through Week 12 kickoff.
- 2014: Trades involving starters required 4-hour pre-kickoff processing; 24 hours to get under cap after trade.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether 2014 veto-poll mechanics are fully replaced by immediate processing with review.

history_events.title: Topic History: Contract Management
history_events.summary: Rookie contract length rule shifted from 2 years (2012) to 3 years (2013) and was removed prior to 2025 (see Rookie Draft section).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Rookie contract length rule shifted from 2 years (2012) to 3 years (2013) and was removed prior to 2025 (see Rookie Draft section).
- MYM (Mid-Year Multi) limit increased from 2 per season (2018) to 3 per season (current).
- Legacy docs include franchise/transition tag systems with compensation rules; current rulebook does not mention tags.
- Legacy docs use 20% cap-penalty on remaining salary; current uses 75% guarantee with monthly earning schedule.
- Legacy docs allow contract restructures (often capped at 2 per season); current mentions restructures but not limits.
- 2012 poll adopted off-season extension timing where raises apply only to extension years (not immediately).
- 2014 forum vote (Feb 11, 2014; passed 7–5) allowed restructures only when an extension is granted; extension cost can be incorporated into the current year and split evenly or front/back loaded.
- 2014 forum clarification (Jun 9, 2013) and vote outcome (Feb 11, 2014 tie) reaffirmed cap of six 3-year contracts, excluding rookie contracts.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Contract details were maintained in a separate Contract Guide (not included in the 2014 PDF).
- 2014 (forum vote 7–5 on 2014-02-11): Restructures allowed only when an extension is granted. Owners may incorporate the extension cost into the current year and may split the total charge evenly across remaining years or front/back load it; payout timing can change but total owed remains the same.
- 2014 (forum clarification 2013-06-09; vote tie on 2014-02-11): Max of six 3-year contracts on a roster; rookie contracts do not count toward the six.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether franchise/transition tags and compensation rules are still in effect (missing from current).
- Confirm restructure limits (e.g., max per season) and whether mid-season acquisitions can still be restructured.
- Confirm whether the 2014 extension-based restructure rule (passed 7–5 on Feb 11, 2014) is still the governing standard and whether any per-team limit applies.
- Confirm whether the six 3-year contract cap (rookies excluded) remains active and if any contract-year cap was ever adopted.
- 2013 forum debate questioned whether owners who acquire back-loaded contracts should face the same cap penalty when cutting (vs. relief for secondary owners); confirm the intended rule and whether any exceptions exist.

history_events.title: Topic History: League Financing
history_events.summary: Dues timing changed from Aug 1 deadline in 2014 to split payments due by auction and Thanksgiving (current).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Dues timing changed from Aug 1 deadline in 2014 to split payments due by auction and Thanksgiving (current).
- Payout structure appears updated for 2024 (current) versus 2014 amounts.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: League dues $50 (returning) and $60 (new owners); payouts: Champion $255, 2nd $125, 3rd $50, Divisional champs $25 each.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Confirm whether any legacy dues deadlines (e.g., Aug 1) still apply.

history_events.title: Topic History: Penalties and Miscellaneous League Rules
history_events.summary: Current penalties section is marked as under review with placeholders; legacy docs provide concrete warning and fine structures.
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Current penalties section is marked as under review with placeholders; legacy docs provide concrete warning and fine structures.
- Legacy special-circumstance rules (holdouts, suspensions, jail, retirements) are more detailed than the current placeholders.
- 2012 Philip Rivers drop reversal established a precedent for reversing immediate drops when reported promptly; waiver-bid mistakes were still treated separately.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- 2014: Warning/fine structure for lineup and nomination issues (2nd warning $10, 3rd +$20, 4th +$30 with lockout).
- 2014: Trade response strikes (4 days in-season, 2 weeks off-season); 3 strikes removes trading privileges.
- 2014: Replacement owners may cut 1 player penalty-free and are relieved of prior cap hits; multi-owner replacement uses dispersal draft rules.

### Unresolved Follow-Ups
- Penalty amounts and appeal process are placeholders; requires authoritative update.
- 2014 forum proposal to run MFL pools (Survivor/Confidence/Pick Em) with a draft-pick prize (e.g., 1.13/2.13) was deferred on 2014-09-03; confirm whether any pool prize rules were later adopted.

history_events.title: Topic History: Scoring Settings
history_events.summary: Punter scoring changed after 2011: 2011 used gross punt yards (PNY) plus punts inside 20 at 2 points; starting in 2012 the league removed net-yards scoring and moved to punts inside 20 at 4 points plus punt-average bonus tiers (ANY).
history_events.body_md:
### Change Notes
- Punter scoring changed after 2011: 2011 used gross punt yards (PNY) plus punts inside 20 at 2 points; starting in 2012 the league removed net-yards scoring and moved to punts inside 20 at 4 points plus punt-average bonus tiers (ANY).
- Return yardage scoring (UY/KY) begins in 2012 at 0.05 per punt return yard and 0.025 per kickoff return yard.

### Legacy Rules Not In Current Rulebook
- No legacy-only notes captured.

open_items.title: MFL Roster Size Versus Written Active-Roster Limits
open_items.summary: Live MFL 2026 settings show rosterSize 50, while the written rulebook still uses active-roster minimum and maximum language centered on 27, 30, and 35.
open_items.body_md:
### Conflicting statements
- Live MFL 2026 platform setting: rosterSize = 50.
- Written rulebook: minimum active roster size is 27, maximum is 35 during the auction and 30 after the contract deadline.

### Why this remains open
- The live platform appears to describe total stored roster capacity, while the written rulebook describes active-roster policy. The two should not be flattened into one number without commissioner confirmation.

### Recommended current handling
- Display both numbers separately, use live MFL settings for platform behavior, and use the written 27/30/35 language as the active-roster policy until resolved.
open_items.needs_confirmation_reason: MFL total roster capacity and written active-roster policy are not expressed the same way.
